examples.title: ER to Relational Mapping - Strong Entity
examples.description: Converting a strong entity with different attribute types to a relational table
examples.explanation: Strong entities map directly to tables. Simple and composite attributes become columns. Multi-valued attributes become separate tables with foreign key reference. Derived attributes are not stored.

examples.title: ER to Relational Mapping - Weak Entity
examples.description: Mapping a weak entity with identifying relationship to tables
examples.explanation: Weak entities map to tables with composite primary key formed by owner's primary key and weak entity's partial key. Foreign key references the owner table. CASCADE delete maintains referential integrity.
examples.spaceComplexity: Dependent table size typically much smaller than Employee

examples.title: ER to Relational Mapping - Binary Relationships
examples.description: Mapping 1:1, 1:N, and M:N relationships to tables
examples.explanation: 1:1: Foreign key can be on either side (usually where participation is total). 1:N: Foreign key goes on the 'many' side. M:N: Requires junction table with combined primary key and foreign keys to both tables.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for basic queries, O(n²) for join-heavy queries on M:N
examples.spaceComplexity: Junction table stores M×N pairs maximum

examples.title: ER Diagram Validation - Check Entity Attributes
examples.description: Python program to validate basic ER modeling rules
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates basic ER model validation rules: entities must have attributes and keys, relationships must have valid cardinalities. Useful for checking database designs programmatically.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - small fixed memory for objects
